standard_refs.title: MQTT Version 3.1.1
standard_refs.notes: Core pilot standard for ClientId, Remaining Length, QoS, SUBACK, and CONNECT behavior.

standard_refs.title: MQTT Version 5.0
standard_refs.notes: Useful for property, reason-code, and enhanced session/error handling cases.

reason_for_selection: Directly aligns with the ProtocolGuard ClientId motivation example.
Small message grammar and easy input construction make it suitable for the first T2/T7 trial.
Several independent implementations are available for cross-implementation validation.

potential_implicit_constraint_points.description: Long ClientId support must not silently collapse distinct identities.
A malformed or non-CONNECT initial packet must not drive unbounded state allocation.
Duplicate or incomplete QoS 2 transactions must not leak or duplicate state.
Reason-code/result list cardinality must be validated before callback access.

notes: MQTT is not an IETF RFC protocol, but it is the best first pilot for reproducing the implicit-constraint idea.
